user_query:
I need a setup plan and installation report for our startup development toolkit across our team's editors. Please read all inputs under input/ and produce two outputs:

1) Write output/summary.json with exactly these fields:
- installed_via: string, must be one of "Method A", "Method B", or "Method C". Honor the preferred method from input/preferences.json and do not override it even if some modules are unavailable.
- modules_total: integer, the number of modules listed in input/modules.json
- unavailable: array of strings, the modules that are unavailable under the chosen method based on input/compatibility.csv (sort this list alphabetically)
- fallbacks: object mapping each unavailable module to a fallback method; use "Method A" as the only fallback method for any unavailable module (include all unavailable modules here)
- server_configured: boolean, mirror input/preferences.json enable_server
- quick_starts: array with at least 3 command strings starting with "/"; include "/research", "/validate", and "/stream"

2) Write output/install_report.md with a clear, human-readable plan that includes all of the following sections (use these exact section labels so it’s easy to find them):
- Title line: "Startup Toolkit Setup Report"
- "Installation method:" followed by the chosen method (e.g., Method B)
- "Modules detected:" followed by the total count
- "Server configured:" yes/no reflecting the preference
- "Unavailable modules:" list the modules not available under the chosen method (alphabetical)
- "Fallback plan:" explain that unavailable modules will be handled via Method A and list each mapping
- "Quick start commands:" include at least "/research", "/validate", and "/stream" (you may add more)

Rules:
- Read input/modules.json, input/preferences.json, and input/compatibility.csv from the input/ directory.
- Do not change the preferred method from input/preferences.json. If some modules are unavailable under that method, list them under “Unavailable modules” and map them to "Method A" under “Fallback plan" and in output/summary.json.
- Ensure output/summary.json and output/install_report.md stay consistent with each other.
- Keep explanations concise but clear; the report should briefly justify choices and outline next steps for the team.
